ground_truth.title: Introduction to Cloud Computing - GeeksforGeeks
ground_truth.author: kartik
ground_truth.main_content:
Cloud Computing has fundamentally changed how businesses and individuals store, access, and manage data. It is a technology that allows you to use data and applications over the internet, moving beyond the limitations of a local computer's hard drive or a private server.

Before the cloud, if a company wanted to launch a website or an application, it had to follow a slow and expensive process. The company had to buy powerful physical servers, storage disks, and networking equipment. This required a huge upfront investment, known as Capital Expenditure (CapEx). They had to guess their peak traffic needs, often buying far more capacity than they used day-to-day, leading to wasted resources.

Cloud Computing is a technology that allows you to store and access data and applications over the internet instead of using your computer's hard drive or a local server.

Cloud computing architecture refers to the components and sub-components required for cloud computing. These components typically include Front end (Fat client, Thin client), Back-end platforms (Servers, Storage), and Cloud-based delivery and a network (Internet, Intranet, Intercloud).

The Front End consists of Thin clients that use web browsers facilitating portable and lightweight accessibilities and Fat Clients that use many functionalities for offering a strong user experience. The Back-end platforms are where the core of cloud computing is made with several servers for storage and processing computing.

All cloud services share five fundamental characteristics that define them: On-Demand Self-Service where users can provision computing resources automatically; Broad Network Access where capabilities are available over the network; Resource Pooling where the provider's computing resources are pooled to serve multiple customers; Rapid Elasticity where resources can be scaled up or down quickly; and Measured Service where usage is monitored and reported.

Security in the cloud is a partnership between the cloud provider and the customer, known as the Shared Responsibility Model. The Cloud Provider is responsible for the security OF the cloud including physical security, hardware, and core networking infrastructure. The Customer is responsible for security IN the cloud including managing access, encrypting data, and securing applications.

The types of Cloud Computing services are: Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) which gives access to IT tools like virtual computers, storage, and networks; Platform as a Service (PaaS) where a provider offers software and hardware tools for development; Software as a Service (SaaS) which is a way of using software over the internet hosted by a company; and Function as a Service (FaaS) which allows customers to run code in response to events without managing infrastructure.

Cloud Deployment Models include: Private Cloud operated solely for a single organization; Public Cloud owned and operated by a third-party cloud service provider; Hybrid Cloud combining private cloud with public clouds; and Multi-Cloud using a combination of clouds from different public cloud providers.

Top leading cloud computing companies include: Amazon with AWS offering Compute, Storage, AI/ML, Databases, Networking; Microsoft with Azure offering Cloud computing, AI, Analytics, Hybrid Cloud; Google with Google Cloud Platform offering AI/ML, Big Data, Kubernetes; Alibaba with Alibaba Cloud offering IaaS, AI, Big Data, Cloud Security; and others like Oracle Cloud, IBM Cloud, Salesforce Cloud, and Tencent Cloud.

Cloud security best practices include Data Encryption to ensure data remains unreadable to unauthorized users; Access Control to ensure only authorized users can access sensitive data; and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) which adds an extra layer of security.

Use cases of Cloud Computing include: Scalable Websites and Applications to host applications that can handle millions of users; Data Storage, Backup, and Recovery; Big Data Analytics; Software Development and Testing; and Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.
with: Cloud Computing has fundamentally changed how businesses and individuals store, access, and manage data.
The cloud changes this model entirely by shifting the cost from a large upfront investment to a manageable monthly bill.
Cloud Computing is a technology that allows you to store and access data and applications over the internet.
The 5 Core Characteristics of Cloud Computing are On-Demand Self-Service, Broad Network Access, Resource Pooling, Rapid Elasticity, and Measured Service.
Security in the cloud is a partnership between the cloud provider and the customer, known as the Shared Responsibility Model.
